title: anthemis arvensis subsp. arvensis
intro: Anthemis arvensis subsp. arvensis, also known as Corn Marigold or Field Calendula, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that grows in fields and meadows. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Anthemis arvensis subsp. arvensis include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting digestive health. The plant is typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a popular ingredient in herbal medicine and skincare products.
